BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Habitat for Humanity Intl Yuba Sutter

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: We are a Christian housing ministry based on the goal of providing decent housing for all people, regardless of race or creed.
IRS990.MissionDesc: We are a Christian housing ministry based on the goal of providing decent housing for all people, regardless of race or creed.
IRS990.Desc: Other Programs:Family services works directly with partner families finding solutions to move into a new home or get necessary repair.Life Building Center program provides self-sufficiency, from medical services to education and casework, homeless services including permanent supportive housing shelter.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: The organizations retail recycling facility, the ReStore, sells quality donated building materials at greatly discounted rates to all members of the general public. This project should allow low-income homeowners, in particular, the opportunity to save their homes from disrepair. The materials collected and funds raised increase the organizations home building capacity.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: The organization builds affordable housing for qualified low income families in the Yuba Sutter Counties. The income of qualified families falls between 30% to 50% of the Countys AMI (as determined by HUD).Habitat for Humanity Yuba/Sutter supports the building of homes for families in need by Habitat for Humanity International.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization is a nonprofit entity exempt from income tax under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code. It has been classified under Section 509(a)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code as a Publicly Supported Organization (not a private foundation), qualifying for the 50% charitable contribution deduction for individual donors. The Organization did not have unrelated business income during the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024.The Organization's federal and state information returns are subject to examination by regulatory agencies. At June 30, 2023, the Organization's federal tax returns related to the preceding three years remain open to examination by tax authorities. No tax returns are currently under examination by any tax authorities and the Organization has not incurred any penalties or interest. Management of the Organization believes it has not taken a tax position that, if challenged, would have a material effect on the financial statements.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part XI, Line 2d: Other revenue amounts included in F/S but not included on form 990
Part XII, Line 2d: Other expenses and losses per audited F/S
Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The CEO provides a copy of the 990 to the board for review prior to submission.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, AND STAFF ARE SUBJECT TO A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY INCORPORATED INTO THE PERSONNEL PLAN. ADHERENCE TO AND MONITORING OF THE POLICY IS THE RESPONSIBILITY OF EACH EMPLOYEE, THEIR SUPERVISOR, AND THE HUMAN RESOURCES DEPARTMENT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Form 990, governing documents, and policies are available for public inspection at the business office.
